description: Congressman Brad Sherman (CA-30) seeks highly motivated, professional, and dedicated fall interns for his D.C. office. Strong oral and written skills, close attention to detail, and a passion for government and politics is imperative. Tasks include answering the phones, addressing constituent concerns, and supporting legislative staff as needed. Ties to California's 30th Congressional District are a plus, but not required. This is a paid, full-time internship.
qualifications: Strong oral and written skills
Close attention to detail
Passion for government and politics
Ties to California's 30th Congressional District are a plus
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should e-mail a cover letter and resume to Brads.Interns@mail.house.gov.

office: Congressman Steve Cohen (TN-09), Chairman of the Constitution, Civil Rights and Civil Liberties Subcommittee
description:
Congressman Steve Cohen (TN-09), Chairman of the Constitution, Civil Rights and Civil Liberties Subcommittee, is accepting applications for a full-time legal fellowship position in his Washington, D.C. office.

Positions are available starting October 1, 2021, through December 31, 2021, with an opportunity for an extension. This fellowship will provide a valuable, hands-on experience in a Congressional office and a greater understanding of the legislative process. Fellows will work closely with an active legislative team and will be expected to produce high-level content under tight deadlines.

Fellows should expect to work on a variety of issue areas including matters before the House Judiciary Committee. This is an excellent opportunity for recent law school graduates seeking substantive Capitol Hill experience. No undergraduate students will be considered. Tennessee ties are preferred, but not required.
responsibilities: Assist staff with preparation for Committee hearings
qualifications: Excellent research and writing skills
Ability to perform under pressure
Interest in the legislative process
Professional demeanor and a collegial attitude
Recent law school graduate
No undergraduate students will be considered
Tennessee ties are preferred, but not required
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email a resume, cover letter, a short writing sample and references to Cohen.jobs@mail.house.gov with "Legal Fellowship" in the subject line. Please, no calls or walk-ins.
salary_info: A stipend will be provided to qualifying fellows.

location: St. Louis District Office or Remote
description:
The office of Congresswoman Cori Bush (MO-01) is seeking a Press/Digital intern to join a dynamic team intent on doing the most for everyone in St. Louis, starting with those who have the very least.

The Press/Digital Intern will build press lists, draft social media posts for the Member's official social media pages (including Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram), assist with press clips, design graphics, and create short video clips as needed. The intern will be an integral part of the communications team, generating engaging content for the Member and supplying creative ideas throughout the week. Ideal applicants will have a background in the Adobe creative suite.

Women, people of color, LGBTQIA+, unhoused people, formerly incarcerated individuals, and members of other marginalized communities are encouraged to apply.

LOCATION: St. Louis District office or Remote
COMPENSATION: $15/hr (15 to 20 hours per week)
START DATE: September 6, 2021
END DATE: December 17, 2021
*Dates subject to change/availability
responsibilities: Draft social media posts for the Member's official social media pages (including Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram)
Create short video clips as needed
Generate engaging content for the Member
Supply creative ideas throughout the week
qualifications: Background in the Adobe creative suite
Ability to work 15 to 20 hours per week
Interest in communications and digital media
salary_info: $15 per hour (15 to 20 hours per week)
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, criminal history or parental status.

office: Fiscally and Socially Conservative Midwestern member of the House (Unresolved)
description: Fiscally and Socially Conservative Midwestern member of the House seeks motivated, detail-oriented, and energetic professionals for internships in Washington, D.C.
responsibilities: Perform internship duties within the office
qualifications: Strong interpersonal skills
Ability to manage multiple tasks
Strong writing skills
Solid work ethic
Missouri/Midwestern ties preferred but not required
how_to_apply: Please send all applicant resumes to Madelyn.derks@mail.house.gov

description:
The internship is for the Member's Washington, DC office and will run through the fall semester. General hours are 9am - 5pm when out of session and 9am - 6pm in session.

The internship is a great way to learn about the inner workings of Capitol Hill, gain an introductory knowledge of the legislative process, and network with others. Duties include: answering phones and talking with constituents, researching various topics to assist the legislative/policy team, running errands, helping to craft letters and general office support.
responsibilities: Answering phones and talking with constituents
Researching various topics to assist the legislative/policy team
Helping to craft letters and general office support
qualifications: Availability for fall semester internship
Ability to work general hours 9am - 5pm when out of session and 9am - 6pm in session
Interest in learning about the inner workings of Capitol Hill and the legislative process
how_to_apply: Please email your resume and cover letter to will.burger@mail.house.gov

description:
The Office of Congressman Sam Graves (MO-06) is seeking a full or part-time paid intern for Fall 2021 in his Washington, D.C. office.

Responsibilities include aiding in constituent services (drafting letters and answering phone calls), researching legislation, attending hearings and lecture series, and leading tours of the U.S. Capitol.

This opportunity is open to college students who are at least 18 years of age.

Applicants should have a strong interest in U.S. politics, a desire to serve the public, excellent customer service skills, and a positive attitude. Connections to the district or Missouri are preferred but not required.
responsibilities: Aiding in constituent services (drafting letters and answering phone calls)
Leading tours of the U.S. Capitol
qualifications: College students who are at least 18 years of age
Strong interest in U.S. politics
Desire to serve the public
Excellent customer service skills
Positive attitude
Connections to the district or Missouri are preferred but not required
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should send their resume, availability, and a brief cover letter to Chase.Hite@mail.house.gov with the subject line: Fall 2021 Internship.

description:
U.S. Representative David N. Cicilline is seeking part-time, remote legal interns for the fall 2021 semester to work with his legislative team.

Interns will work closely with the office's Legislative Counsel and other staff, and duties will entail conducting legal research, drafting materials related to hearings and votes, and providing other assistance as needed. Issue areas will include constitutional law, oversight over federal agencies, criminal justice, gun violence prevention, voting rights, immigration, intellectual property, privacy, financial services, and women's issues/reproductive rights.

This internship is open to current 2L and 3L law students in good academic standing. Students must be able to receive academic credit for the internship and are expected to work 2-3 days per week. This position will be completely remote but will entail frequent communication and collaboration with Legislative Counsel and other staff.

Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, individuals with disabilities, and those with Rhode Island ties are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Draft materials related to hearings and votes
Communicate and collaborate with Legislative Counsel and other staff
qualifications: Current 2L or 3L law student in good academic standing
Ability to receive academic credit for the internship
Availability to work 2-3 days per week
Strong communication skills for remote collaboration
how_to_apply: Email a resume, cover letter, three-to-five-page writing sample, unofficial law school transcript, and list of three references with email contacts to RI01LegalInterns@gmail.com. Send all application documents in a single PDF file and include 'Legal Intern Application' in the subject line.
equal_opportunity: Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, individuals with disabilities, and those with Rhode Island ties are strongly encouraged to apply.

description:
Congressman Dan Meuser (PA-09) seeks applicants for part-time and full-time paid internships for Fall 2021 in his Washington, D.C. office.

Interns will assist with legislative and administrative tasks by conducting research, answering phones, leading tours, drafting letters, among other tasks.

Pennsylvania ties are preferred but not required.
responsibilities: Assist with legislative and administrative tasks
qualifications: Pennsylvania ties preferred but not required
Ability to perform legislative and administrative tasks
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should send a cover letter and resume to PA09Jobs@mail.house.gov. Please no drop-ins or phone calls.

Ideal candidates have strong writing skills, a good work ethic, an interest in learning about House floor procedures, and are detail-oriented. Applicants must have a positive attitude and be willing to perform a variety of tasks.
responsibilities: Learn about the Rules of the House and the special-order process
Complete a variety of other administrative tasks
qualifications: Strong writing skills
Good work ethic
Interest in learning about House floor procedures
Detail-oriented
Positive attitude
Willing to perform a variety of tasks

how_to_apply: Interested applicants can submit their application online at https://forms.office.com/g/TV5XdH47vb. No calls or drop-ins please.

description: Congressman Alexander X. Mooney (WV-02) is currently seeking internship applicants in the D.C. office for Fall of 2021. Qualified applicants should possess strong communication skills, strong writing skills, and be interested in learning about the legislative process.
responsibilities: Conducting research and additional tasks to assist the staff
qualifications: Strong communication skills
Strong writing skills
Interest in learning about the legislative process
West Virginia ties preferred but not required
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should send resume, cover letter and availability to gop1863interns@gmail.com. Closing date 2021-09-17.

office: House Committee on the Judiciary (Democratic Staff)
description:
The House Judiciary Committee Democratic staff are looking for interns for the spring semester.

The House Committee on the Judiciary's staff internship program offers undergraduate students, law students, graduate students, and recent graduates an opportunity to experience the operation of a congressional committee firsthand and learn about the important role that our Committee plays in the legislative process.

The Judiciary Committee has jurisdiction over a wide range of legislative and oversight issues, including oversight of the U.S. Department of Justice, constitutional law, federal criminal law, voting rights, immigration and naturalization, intellectual property law, and antitrust law. Stipends will be available to spring interns.

Intern assignments will vary depending on the work of the Judiciary Committee each semester.
responsibilities: Researching policy issues under the committee's jurisdiction
Attending briefings and hearings on the Hill
Supporting committee clerks and professional staff members
Providing administrative support, including answering calls, delivering correspondence, and setting up the hearing room
qualifications: Undergraduate students, law students, graduate students, and recent graduates
Interest in legislative and oversight issues including constitutional law, federal criminal law, voting rights, immigration, intellectual property, and antitrust law
how_to_apply: The deadline to apply for the spring semester is October 1, 2021. This internship is based in Washington, DC. To learn more and apply, please visit judiciary.house.gov/about/internship-opportunities.htm
equal_opportunity: The Committee strives to ensure that our internship class reflects the diversity of the country's population; candidates from minority backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. The Committee does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin.
